input.question: We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. Also attached is an image showing seven CAD views (each boxed in black) of our vehicle design. The top, big view shows a close-up view of the design. The six smaller views on the bottom of the image show different complete views of the CAD of the vehicle and are provided for context. Note that the close-up view orientation matches one of the six complete view orientations. The close-up view may also have some components hidden (with respect to the corresponding complete view) for visualization of specific components. Looking at the close-up view, is/are any part of the the powertrain or the drivetrain visible in the close-up view? Answer simply with yes or no.

retrieved_context:
RULE: t.5.2.1
            TITLE: exposed high speed final drivetrain equipment such as continuously variable transmissions

            CONTENT:
            rule T.5.2.1: Exposed high speed final drivetrain equipment such as Continuously Variable Transmissions
T.5.2.1 Exposed high speed final drivetrain equipment such as Continuously Variable Transmissions 
(CVTs), sprockets, gears, pulleys, torque converters, clutches, belt drives, clutch drives and 
electric motors, must be fitted with scatter shields intended to contain drivetrain parts in case 
of radial failure

RULE: d.2.3
            TITLE: powertrain operation

            CONTENT:
            rule D.2.3: Powertrain Operation
D.2.3 Powertrain Operation 
In the paddock, (IC) Engines may be run or (EV) Tractive System may be Active if all three:   
a. The vehicle has passed Technical Inspection up to and including the Tilt Test OR a 
Technical Inspector gives permission  
b. The vehicle is supported on a stand  
c. The drive wheels are minimum 10 cm off the ground, OR the drive wheels are removed
Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International  Page 126 of 140   
Version 1.0   1 Sep 2023

RULE: t.5.1
            TITLE: transmission and drive

            CONTENT:
            rule T.5.1: Transmission and Drive
T.5.1 Transmission and Drive 
Any transmission and drivetrain may be used.

RULE: ic.5.4.6
            TITLE: the sight tube and fuel level line must be clearly visible to two individuals (one to fill the tank,

            CONTENT:
            rule IC.5.4.6: The sight tube and fuel level line must be clearly visible to two individuals (one to fill the tank,
IC.5.4.6 The sight tube and fuel level line must be clearly visible to two individuals (one to fill the tank, 
the other to visually verify fill) without the need of assistance (artificial lighting, magnifiers, 
etc) or the need to remove any parts (body panels, etc).

RULE: v.3.1.3
            TITLE: all suspension mounting points must be visible at technical inspection by direct view or by

            CONTENT:
            rule V.3.1.3: All suspension mounting points must be visible at Technical Inspection by direct view or by
V.3.1.3 All suspension mounting points must be visible at Technical Inspection by direct view or by 
removing any covers.
